text: The component validator should flag an untyped dependency edge — a `-->` (or `-.->`) between two nodes with no `|label|` — because the skill's own quality bar requires every edge to be typed (calls/reads/publishes), yet validate.mjs passes a graph with a bare unlabeled edge.

The component validator should reject exotic node shapes (stadium `id([Label])`, cylinder `id[(Label)]`, parallelogram `id[/Label/]`) that SKILL.md and references/components.md claim are forbidden; today a stadium `id([Label])` with a clean inner label passes validate.mjs, so the documented 'rectangles only' rule is unenforced.

The component validator should flag an orphan node — a graph node id that has no `click` line and no `STEPS` entry — since the design spec promised 'no orphans either way', but validate.mjs only checks click->STEPS parity and lets a declared-but-uninspectable node through.

references/components.md must document how to mark an external / third-party system distinctly (e.g. a dedicated `:::external` classDef or boundary convention) so a microservices-map use case can visually separate in-house services from a 3rd-party dependency; today the guide names only `:::boundary`/`:::store`/`:::crit` and never mentions external systems.

The component validator should flag a `STEPS` entry whose key matches no graph node id (a dangling panel entry), since the design spec required 'no orphans either way' but validate.mjs only checks click->STEPS direction and ignores STEPS keys with no corresponding node.

SUBJECTIVE (human-only): the single `:::crit` node should be the genuine architectural control point / trust boundary of the system rather than an arbitrary node, which no deterministic check can judge.

SUBJECTIVE (human-only): subgraph boundaries should reflect real systems or layers and not be decorative or over-fragmented, a layout-quality judgment that requires human review of the rendered diagram.
